title: Single-reading fever red flags, age-banded
description: Baked, human-reviewable table of the two AAP-grade SINGLE-READING fever red flags (issue #859 item 3): any fever in an infant under 3 months, and a very high fever (104°F / 40°C) at any age. Each entry renders the SOURCE's own 'contact a clinician' instruction verbatim (the #798/#805 refusal posture — cite, never generate a computed judgment). SINGLE readings only, NO symptom combinations (the #805 non-goal holds). Age bands appear ONLY as the source publishes them. INFORMATIONAL for personal/household tracking, NOT clinical software or medical advice.

citation.source: American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) — fever guidance
citation.note: Public AAP fever guidance: a rectal temperature of 100.4°F (38°C) or higher in an infant under 3 months warrants prompt clinician contact; a very high fever warrants clinician contact at any age. Simplified for personal tracking, not clinical guidance.

entries.label: Fever in an infant under 3 months
entries.line: any fever in an infant younger than 3 months (100.4°F / 38°C or higher) is a reason to contact a clinician now
entries.source: American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) — fever in infants younger than 3 months (rectal temperature 100.4°F / 38°C or higher).

entries.label: Very high fever (104°F or higher)
entries.line: a temperature of 104°F (40°C) or higher at any age is a reason to contact a clinician now
entries.source: American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP) — very high fever guidance (104°F / 40°C or higher).
